title: Ensure that the cluster enforces Pod Security Standard  Baseline profile or stricter for all namespaces.

input_row.title: Ensure that the cluster enforces Pod Security Standard  Baseline profile or stricter for all namespaces.
input_row.description: The Pod Security Standard Baseline profile defines a baseline for container security. You can enforce this by using the built-in Pod Security Admission controller.
input_row.rationale: Without an active mechanism to enforce the Pod Security Standard Baseline profile, it is not possible to limit the use of containers with access to underlying cluster nodes, via mechanisms like privileged containers, or the use of hostPath volume mounts.
input_row.audit: Run the following command to list the namespaces that don't have the the baseline policy enforced. diff \ <(kubectl get namespace -l pod-security.kubernetes.io/enforce=baseline -o jsonpath='{range .items[*]}{.metadata.name}{"\n"}') \ <(kubectl get namespace -o jsonpath='{range .items[*]}{.metadata.name}{"\n"}')
input_row.remediation: Ensure that Pod Security Admission is in place for every namespace which contains user workloads. Run the following command to enforce the Baseline profile in a namespace: kubectl label namespace pod- security.kubernetes.io/enforce=baseline
input_row.impact: Enforcing a baseline profile will limit the use of containers.
input_row.default_value: By default, Pod Security Admission is enabled but no policies are in place. Internal Only - General

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
The control regarding enforcing the Pod Security Standard Baseline profile can be fully verified using GCP APIs. The audit command provided in the JSON confirms the use of `kubectl`, which aligns with GCP’s capabilities in managing Kubernetes environments through APIs. Additionally, GCP CLI commands can also verify namespace configurations. This indicates that the control is not only API-checkable but can be automated using existing tools.

**PROGRAM NAME:** gcp_gke_cluster_pod_security_standard_baseline_enforcement

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:** 
The automated program would check the following:

- **GCP API calls or Google Cloud SDK (gcloud) methods to use:** 
  The program would utilize the Kubernetes API to evaluate the enforcement of Pod Security Policies in the GKE clusters.

- **GCP CLI commands (if applicable):** 
  Use `kubectl` commands to list namespaces and check for the Pod Security Standard enforcement:
  ```
  kubectl get namespaces --show-labels
  ```

- **Specific resources to enumerate:** 
  - GKE namespaces within the cluster.

- **Exact configuration properties/settings to validate:** 
  The relevant configuration property is the `pod-security.kubernetes.io/enforce` label on namespaces.

- **Clear Pass/Fail criteria with expected values:** 
  - **Pass:** All namespaces should either have the label `pod-security.kubernetes.io/enforce=baseline` or a stricter version applied.
  - **Fail:** Any namespace lacking the appropriate label indication requires remediation.

This automated check will confirm which namespaces have the Baseline profile enforced or stricter, ensuring compliance with the CIS control as stated in the provided documentation.
